translation: as the sugar on the bottom
meaning: As a final bonus, benefit or gift

translation: (to be) a person with black socks
meaning: to be (possessively) jealous of someone (a person)

translation: where people are around (lit. in people's air/weather)
meaning: At a place popular or populated enough to earn one's respect. Can also be used more literally, to mean at a place where one can be rescued.

translation: now Lyyti begins to write
meaning: Something is finally succeeding and one can proceed.

translation: so the branches fall off (the tree)
meaning: used to describe almost anything (mostly anger) in excessive quantity. also used in conjunction with the subject of immediate action

translation: to cross sticks
meaning: To perform the most minimal effort. Cf. English "lift a finger". Nearly always used with a negative.

translation: lit. ruffe of the opposite shore, from kiiski (Gymnocephalus cernuus). A spiky fish that offers great resistance to the fishing line, in this case by anchoring it to the trees growing on the opposite shore of the lake.
meaning: Someone in constant opposition and immune to any reasoning

translation: like a bear which has been shot in the ass
meaning: Being extremely cranky.

idiom: konstit on monet (, sanoi akka, kun kissalla pöytää pyyhki)
translation: the ways are many (said the woman while wiping the table with a cat)
meaning: There's more than one way to do something. Often used when it becomes necessary to resort to unconventional methods. (cf. "there is more than one way to skin a cat.")

translation: on the wide road
meaning: To live outside the law (possible Biblical metaphor).

translation: (take) behind the sauna
meaning: "To put off/execute". Due to fire hazard, saunas were built at a distance from the house, and what was or happened behind the sauna was out of sight, "behind the backyard". Normally, there was a "tunkio" or a garbage pile behind the sauna, and when e.g. a sick or injured horse or dog needed to be put down, it was taken behind the sauna for the job.

translation: by throwing
meaning: With little or no effort required.

translation: one's moped starts doing wheelies
meaning: Ideas or other actions start to get out of control.

translation: donkey's bridge; from Latin pons asinorum
meaning: A tenuous connection between an old discussion topic and a new one.

translation: to learn by way of the heel
meaning: Learn a lesson the hard way

translation: looks like one's mother
meaning: Not looking good at all. Comes from an allegation of being the son of a whore.

translation: there are two ends to a sausage
meaning: Two sides of a thing, cf. "two sides of a coin"

idiom: yhdeksän hyvää ja kymmenen kaunista, yhdeksän hyvää ja kahdeksan kaunista
translation: nine good things and ten beautiful things, nine good things and eight beautiful things
meaning: Lots of good-sounding promises, with an implication they are unlikely to be kept.

translation: pull a pea up one's nose
meaning: To be provoked.

translation: into the well of Kankkula
meaning: To go to waste.

translation: comes loose like grandma's tooth
meaning: Goes very, very easily.

translation: in a coming year, in the birch bark month
meaning: At an indeterminate point in the future, possibly never. "Tuohikuu" is a fictional month but sounds like the rural-oriented Finnish names for the 12 months.

translation: (pay the) learning costs
meaning: Learn a lesson the hard way

translation: to slip away from the (woollen) glove
meaning: To go out of control.

translation: (as if) urinated while running
meaning: Haphazardly implemented.

translation: to go into the forest (in something)
meaning: To be misguided, to go wrong; usually used to describe unintended consequences of decisions or plans

translation: one's nose was not wheezing for long
meaning: Something happened that was over very quickly.

translation: to throw one's crankshaft
meaning: To die.

translation: to cost honey/malts/strawberries
meaning: to cost very much, to be very expensive.

translation: to paint a devil on the wall
meaning: to expect a misfortune to happen by speaking about it and the chance of it happening.

translation: under a stone
meaning: Difficult to obtain

translation: to think about the crows in the sky
meaning: To not pay attention to what's going on.

translation: from the ditch to the duck pond
meaning: out of the frying pan into the fire, from one bad situation to another

translation: To not have a mouth of birch bark
meaning: To not abstain from food or drink (always used with a negative).

translation: to Nevada (for all I care) to the horse's sprucetree
meaning: Away, to an unknown place, never to be seen again. While the name Nevada is an obvious reference to the U.S. state, "huitsin" probably refers to Hiisi.

translation: (to sit and) contemplate the deep origins
meaning: To meditate on the mysteries of the world; used ironically, to do nothing (from Finnish mythology).

translation: against the walls / tree / pines / ...
meaning: Completely wrong.

translation: to move to a different diocese
meaning: To die.

translation: as if swallowed a spear
meaning: Standing unnaturally or needlessly upright. To be turgid and bumptious. To be seemingly nervous.

translation: having one’s jacket open
meaning: Approaching a demanding task unprepared and with careless attitude

translation: to keep opening one's head
meaning: (Someone) speaking in a brash or reckless manner. Yapping.

translation: soitellen, archaic for travel, related to Estonian sõita (not to be confuced with playing an instrument) sotaan, to war
meaning: To be unprepared, whether it is a task at hand or one is going somewhere without thinking what is needed to take along.

translation: to speak holes into (one's) head
meaning: To make statements without requisite knowledge. Often used of one's own words Cf. English "I may be speaking out of my ass"

translation: go into the tube (or pipe)
meaning: To succeed, to work as planned.

translation: a buck (male goat) as a guard of a cabbage patch
meaning: someone with a conflict of interest in a position of responsibility

translation: to kick empty space
meaning: To die. Cf. English "to kick the bucket"

translation: like ducks (exactly: a particular species (Long-tailed Duck) that leaves last in the autumn) on a cliff
meaning: Left by oneself, without outside support.

translation: to straighten one's legs
meaning: To die.

translation: (someone has) bats in the belfry
meaning: A slow-witted or mentally handicapped person

translation: out like a snowman
meaning: Not to know anything about the a given thing and thus be stunned, or to say things that are not relevant to the thing.

translation: in the birch bark month on the day of the goat
meaning: Same as above.

translation: read like the Devil reads the Bible
meaning: To deliberately look for loopholes, in order to follow the letter without following the spirit. Note: Based on the Bible (Luke 4:1-13, Matthew 4:1-11 and Psalms 91:11-12). English equivalent: The devil can cite scripture for his purpose. (Shakespeare, The Merchant of Venice act 1, scene 3)

translation: to take the time of one's age and health
meaning: To take a far too long time, or be of far too much effort.

translation: as H. Moilanen
meaning: Shocked, mouth agape, utterly unprepared, never heard

translation: to come as if coming into a hut made of tree needles
meaning: To enter uninvited.

translation: With the good or the bad.
meaning: Willingly or by force.

translation: to play one's mouth (as a musical instrument)
meaning: To boast, or to speak provocatively.

translation: like a louse in tar
meaning: Extremely slow

translation: like two berries
meaning: Two people or things that seem extremely alike; two peas in a pod.

translation: to leave like the Devil leaves a revival
meaning: To leave extremely fast (also used when an inanimate object is quickly propelled to a great speed)

translation: person does not have all the native Americans (lit. Indians) in the canoe
meaning: Person is not too bright / has mental issues

translation: pot scolds a kettle
meaning: Used to claim that a person is guilty of the very thing of which they accuse another. Cf. English "pot calling kettle black".

translation: with one's mittens straight
meaning: Not doing anything, with an implication that one should be doing something.

translation: in the middle of nowhere
meaning: Calque from English — natively korvessa Cf. English "In the middle of bumfuck Egypt"

translation: to throw the spoon into the corner
meaning: To die or to give up.

translation: let/start to throw/pull
meaning: To tell someone to leave immediately, "Get out/off!/Buzz off! /Take a hike!".

translation: (throw) into the hillslope
meaning: (To throw) away.

translation: (to climb) arse first into a tree
meaning: To do something in a needlessly complicated way, failing to see an easier way

translation: not to spit into the glass
meaning: Not to abstain from alcohol (always used with a negative).

translation: Amazed as a Common cuckoo
meaning: To be confused about something.

translation: the horses are running away
meaning: A euphemism for "your fly is open".

translation: in the year of sword/cone/mashed potatoes and helmet/stone/wiener
meaning: Very long time ago.

translation: to jump onto walls
meaning: To act alarmedly; to panic.

translation: as a piece of trash in the broth
meaning: As the bad exception in an otherwise good company. More commonly used in a positive way, c.f. "every little bit helps".

translation: to pick up one's bones
meaning: To finally get around to leaving.

translation: to leave as if leaving an inn
meaning: To leave without cleaning up after oneself. (holli from Swedish hålla (“to keep”) + tupa).

translation: to show how the revivalists chant (a saying from Upper Savonia and Northern Ostrobothnia, where revivalist movements are strong)
meaning: To show someone how to properly do a thing they are attempting to do.

translation: arse(s) on a shoulder
meaning: very drunk

translation: be in a 'loss'; also, hukka means "wolf"
meaning: Being lost, missing, in despair, perished.

translation: son of yesterday's grouse
meaning: Inexperienced, gullible (usually used with a negative, "nobody's fool", cf. "born yesterday")

translation: like drinking tar
meaning: Extremely slow, difficult, or arduous.

translation: like a pot of sour milk
meaning: Very calm.

translation: in a phase (back-formation from asking "in which phase?")
meaning: Not yet ready or finished, with an implication of not knowing when it will be. Originally army slang.

translation: to twist from wire (originally used to assemble plumbing)
meaning: To explain with very simple terms.

translation: open
meaning: Without money, broke, to owe money

translation: porridges and gruels mixed up
meaning: Confusing two unrelated things, either for each other, or as relating to each other. Cf. comparing apples and oranges.

translation: person does not have all the Moomins in the valley
meaning: Person is not too bright / has mental issues

translation: a thumb in the middle of a palm
meaning: Very bad at doing something, usually manual labour. Cf. English "all thumbs".

translation: raining as from Esteri's ass
meaning: Raining heavily. Raining cats and dogs.

translation: (has) a chicken to pluck
meaning: To have unresolved disputes, cf. English "bone to pick"

translation: long-tailed duck sitting on rock
meaning: This idiom has been misheard as "nalli kalliolla" (explosive primer on rock). The original idiom is "Jäi kuin alli kalliolle", meaning "Was left behind, like long-tailed duck". When migratory birds are leaving in autumn, the long-tailed ducks leaves later than other birds.

translation: This does not resemble distress
meaning: Don't worry. We'll make through this (in a distress/stressful situation)

translation: like a fish without a bicycle
meaning: Perfectly well in its own right; for example "a woman without a husband is like a fish without a bicycle".

translation: to rise/get up from the bed with the wrong foot
meaning: To be very grumpy first thing in the morning when everything seems to go wrong. Similar to English "get up on the wrong side of the bed".

translation: to be a lamb
meaning: a person who is like a lamb does nothing alone. the person does everything that for example the clique he/she belongs to.

translation: in resin
meaning: Having a crush on someone

idiom: Manu on työnsä tehnyt, Manu saa mennä
translation: Manu has done his job, Manu is dismissed
meaning: An ironic expression, when faced with ingratitude after doing a favor. The name "manu" comes from the word for "master", like shoemaker-master. In old times these professionals used to tour the countryside practicing their profession.

translation: like living in a field
meaning: To live without cleaning up after oneself, or bothering about what others think about one.

translation: (it came like) like an evening meal to Manu
meaning: Gaining something good without any effort - just turning up and getting paid. The name "manu" comes from the word for "master", like shoemaker-master. In old times these professionals used to tour the countryside practicing their profession, and the pay was often a meal.

translation: (to get) a long nose
meaning: to get shamed because you’ve lost to someone or been proved wrong

translation: (common sense) frozen
meaning: Being clueless about something or understanding the point slowly.

translation: pull to the muzzle
meaning: To beat someone, especially punch in the face. The verb vetää is a verb with diverse, disparate meanings, used to indicate a sudden jerk or a single, quick movement.

translation: (to disappear) like ash in the wind/ a fart in Sahara
meaning: (To sink) without a trace.

translation: to go over one's dandruff
meaning: To be too difficult for one to comprehend.

translation: for even sheep to eat
meaning: A great number or amount, especially one that will stay great after partaken of.

translation: by the ways of a self surface, selfsurfacely
meaning: insistently.

translation: rye in one's wrist
meaning: Physically strong.

translation: to set oneself crosswise
meaning: To start to object to something. Often used if that something has already been generally agreed on.

translation: with the helmet askew
meaning: Downhearted

translation: I can't change into that
meaning: A statement that a desired object simply isn't available, no matter how much someone keeps asking for it. The word "siksi" (into that) is substituted with the object in question, in translative "-ksi".

translation: it took a barley bread / straw bread (slightly ungrammatical). After losing the rye harvest, one had to sow barley, an inferior crop, before the winter
meaning: To encounter severe problems.

idiom: joko teet tai itket ja teet
translation: either you'll do it or you'll cry and do it
meaning: A direct order to do something, whether the addressed wants to do it or not. The word "teet" ('you do') can be substituted with whatever verb is necessary from context.

translation: to like + some hump (the unusual grammar is hard to translate)
meaning: To dislike. Implies vindictiveness. Often juxtaposed with the antonym.

translation: as full as Turunen's gun
meaning: Excessively full, nothing will fit in any more. Comes from the Great Northern War, where a Finnish soldier called Turunen had stuffed his gun so full of food he couldn't shoot it any more.

translation: who bows to one, will show his butt to the others
meaning: If you favour one, you'll discriminate the others.

translation: in the year one and two
meaning: Very long time ago.

translation: as if swallowed a lemon
meaning: Sulky or (easily) irritated

translation: To run with one's head as a third leg
meaning: To be in a great hurry

translation: (to stand like) the letter Ö (the last letter of the Finnish alphabet) in the corner of the spelling book
meaning: Not having a clue, being useless.

translation: To not see the rest of the forest beyond the trees
meaning: Focusing on minute details, whilst failing to see the bigger picture

translation: as cold as a Russian feels like in hell
meaning: Old Finnish swearing if it's really cold outside. Like in English: it's cold as hell!

translation: in the backwoods; out in the sticks
meaning: A place that is somewhere in an area with no interesting things surrounding it, to be "out in the sticks".

translation: suit ghost
meaning: A person who insists on being sharply and formally dressed, but who appears to do little, if any, actual work. Almost always used of men, but sometimes of women too.

idiom: Kuka kissan hännän nostaa ellei kissa itse?
translation: Who would raise a cat's tail, but the cat himself?
meaning: To toot your own horn or boast about yourself.

translation: like a rooster on top of the garbage pile
meaning: Boasting of oneself with cheap merits, being "king of the hill" when said hill is for example a garbage pile. It is easy as no one else wants to be the king of a garbage hill.

translation: knife-scabbards began to clatter
meaning: There is going to be a fight. Finnish fighting knife, "Helapääpuukko" (Puukko with bone-breaking ferrules) is carried in hardened leather scabbard, and the scabbard mouth has water-tight fit. Before knife-fight starts, fighters need to twist the knife handle to loosen it for quick-drawing. When knife is twisted within scabbard, the blade clatters against the hardened leather, and makes clattering sound.

translation: Beaten to one's head with a mind-blow/Beaten to one's head with a spiked mace
meaning: Also to be amazed of some mind-blowing knowledge/to be confused about something so you couldn't say anything out of your mouth.

translation: to leave like common goldeneye (Bucephala clangula, bird) from the birdhouse
meaning: To leave quickly

translation: open-arsed
meaning: Without money, broke.

translation: towards small ones
meaning: Pregnant. Cf. English "with child"

translation: like the owner of Europe/the world
meaning: Acting excessively posh and snobby. Usually an implication of misplacen superiority towards others.

translation: to put in a yo-yo/ throat-swing
meaning: To execute by hanging. Almost always used of suicide.

translation: to start to pull (vetää, much like English set, has various unrelated meanings, e.g. to lead, to drink alcohol)
meaning: To leave immediately. Take off (leaving).

translation: smile like a "a biscuit from Hanko" (a biscuit brand had an embossed "smiley" on the round biscuits. These biscuits were manufactured in Hanko from 1910 to 1940. The original name of the factory was Suomalais-Englantilainen Biscuittehdas, ‘Finnish–English Biscuit Factory’. Hangon Keksi was also one of the leading advertisers in Finland of its time, and the biscuits were marketed in an exemplary way. The smiling Hangon Keksi biscuit, appearing in constantly changing adverts, was in fact the man on the moon.)
meaning: To smile very broadly and happily.

translation: to have the task or job in one's glove (the gloves are lost)
meaning: to have it under control or to be able to do the task at hand (to not have it under control or not being able to do the task at hand)

translation: Rahikainen has the same problem
meaning: Used as a reply when someone says they are or do something, meaning "so am I" or "so do I". "Rahikainen" refers to the speaker, regardless of what their actual surname is.

translation: behind God's back
meaning: Possibly a calque from the English phrase "a godforsaken place". Sometimes implies a disrespect of rural communities.

translation: to circle like a cat circles hot porridge
meaning: To constantly and obviously covet something without actually making a move to get it, or to try to approach a difficult topic of speech in a roundabout way.

translation: won't result in either a baby or shit
meaning: An unsuccessful, futile or abortive effort.

translation: to show (somebody) the eggs (or, in slang, testicles) of a jay
meaning: To show that one can do something better than the person the "eggs" are to be shown to, OR, to give somebody a good beating.

translation: drunkard's buttons
meaning: When someone buttons up his/her shirt wrong.

translation: Some extra care won't flip a boat
meaning: There's no harm in having some extra resources or precautions

translation: to throw something at waterfowl
meaning: To throw away. (As in throwing into a lake.)

translation: We're not riding on the back of a hare
meaning: Not being in an immediate hurry.

translation: the attic blacked out
meaning: Someone fainted

translation: from the top of a large fish trap
meaning: Caught straight from doing a no-good thing, such as stealing fish from your neighbour's trap. Cf. English "caught red-handed". E.g. the American TV program "Cheaters" is translated to "Rysän päältä" in Finnish.

translation: with one's own cow in the ditch
meaning: Having a personal interest or investment in the matter.

translation: with Porvoo measure
meaning: More than asked for, generously. The Porvoo measure was a standardized measuring cup used by tax men to measure grain.

translation: take a granpa out of situation/take care about a situation
meaning: To take a chance when an opportunity strikes.

translation: to wait as if waiting for the rising moon
meaning: To wait for something very anxiously, to the point of desperation.

translation: to pull cabers / to pull logs
meaning: To sleep.

idiom: kuin lasta löisi / helppoa kuin lapsen lyöminen/hakkaaminen
translation: like beating a child / easy like hitting/beating a child
meaning: Very, very easy.

translation: a dog buried
meaning: An implication that there's something foul behind the scenes, the situation is not what it appears to be.

translation: Death in a wallet
meaning: Broke (without money). The Persian for "dead" is "mat". In Finnish it is pronounced as "matti" as in "Shakki Matti" (Shah Mat). "Minulla on matti kukkarossa" could be translated "My moneypurse is dead". In a similar way "Olen täysin matti" means "I am dead tired".

translation: to go gingerbread
meaning: Something goes completely wrong. Could be translated as "Shatters as gingerbread".

translation: to turn one's sled
meaning: To do an about-face/turnabout.

translation: nose-bleaching
meaning: Sobering up, as in the sense of not drinking alcoholic beverages for a week or a month.

translation: like the Sun over Naantali
meaning: Of a wide-smile face.

translation: from/of the times of Adam
meaning: (Of an object) very old, old-fashioned

translation: ten (points) for the attempt (lit. attempt good ten)
meaning: Good, but failed, attempt.

translation: (yeah, and) cows can fly
meaning: Expressing doubt, skepticism.

translation: (to disappear) like a fart in the Sahara
meaning: (to disappear) without a trace Cf. English "like a fart in the wind"

translation: pissing honey
meaning: Extremely happy & satisfied

translation: to pay as much as to get sick/hurt oneself
meaning: to pay very much, leaving oneself with very little money left over.

translation: to hang up your gloves
meaning: give up

translation: snows of winters past
meaning: Not relevant anymore, often used of past offenses or sorrows, similar in tone to English "let bygones be bygones".

translation: with dark clouds
meaning: A huge (often excessive) amount of something.

idiom: olla pää sitä varten, ettei selkäranka valu housuihin
translation: to (only) have a head to prevent one's spine dropping to one's pants
meaning: To be helplessly stupid.

translation: there would be work for an axe
meaning: Something should be corrected, possibly with quite harsh means.

translation: on the narrow path
meaning: To live within the law (possible Biblical metaphor).
